events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the assessment directly to `/app/answer.json` with the required schema. The key issue is premature removal of temporary bracing before permanent stability, so this has to fail with high life-safety risk.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la /app && cat > /tmp/answer.json.check <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"high\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 70,
  \"value\": 70,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.93,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Temporary bracing was removed before decking or permanent stability was in place.\",
    \"Column and beam frame is not adequately stabilized for rough-complete condition.\",
    \"Erection condition creates collapse, displacement, and worker exposure hazard.\",
    \"Work does not meet structural steel erection stability and safety expectations.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Stop affected erection work and restrict access below and around the unstable frame.\",
    \"Reinstall or provide engineered temporary bracing immediately.\",
    \"Verify plumb, alignment, bolt-up, connections, and frame stability after rebracing.\",
    \"Do not resume decking or follow-on work until permanent stability path or approved temporary stability plan is confirmed.\",
    \"Document reinspection through the quality gate before closeout.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"The steel frame is roughly installed but cannot be accepted because temporary bracing was removed before decking or another permanent lateral stability system was established. This is an installed defective and non-compliant rough-complete condition with significant collapse and worker-safety risk. Treat progress as about 70 percent complete until bracing, stability, plumb, alignment, and inspection defects are corrected.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"AISC 303 - Code of Standard Practice: erection stability, temporary supports, plumb/alignment tolerances, and completion responsibilities\",
    \"OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart R - Steel Erection: structural stability, temporary bracing/guying, and fall/collapse hazard controls\"
  ]
}
EOF
python3 -m json.tool /tmp/answer.json.check > /app/answer.json
cat /app/answer.json"
